page: Amazon Web Services m6id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture server includes 256 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, providing high-speed local data access without relying solely on network-attached storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by its top-quartile results in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU scores, and software compilation tasks. The processor's support for AVX-512 vector extensions and hardware-accelerated cryptography contributes to strong performance in extended instructions and encryption workloads. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier, indicating that workloads relying heavily on single-threaded execution speed may not see the same scaling benefits as highly parallelized tasks.

The server's memory subsystem and storage configuration support demanding data operations, with PassMark database benchmarks scoring in the top 25%. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 45.66 GB/sec. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the integration of substantial local NVMe storage alongside a balanced CPU-to-memory ratio offers high resource density for applications requiring both compute power and fast local scratch space. This makes the instance appropriate for databases, multi-threaded web serving, software build pipelines, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 256 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor on the Nitro hypervisor. It integrates 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with top-quartile performance in multi-core CPU tests, software compilation, and database operations, while single-core performance and memory bandwidth sit in the average tier. The inclusion of local NVMe storage alongside balanced compute resources provides qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive applications. This instance is suited for database hosting, parallelized build pipelines, static web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage for balanced, data-intensive workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose platform combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing with high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256 GB DDR4 RAM powered by a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor.
3800 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed local data access.
Strong multi-core CPU, database operations, and software compilation benchmark performance.
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth supported by the Nitro hypervisor.
Suited for database hosting, multi-threaded web serving, and CI/CD pipelines.
